The intelligence matrix is an analytical tool that allows for the classification of information based on various criteria such as its nature, source, security level, and intended use. It helps structure information and understand its context. By categorizing data, the matrix allows for the determination of the reliability and usefulness of each piece of information. This tool can be used in various fields to organize research, manage sensitive data, and facilitate decision-making. The intelligence matrix offers a methodical framework for processing information efficiently and securely
instructions: The Intelligence Matrix can be used in different ways, depending on your needs. Here is an example method Searching for information on a theme External analysis of this information Analysis using the information matrix Structured understanding of the information Classifying the information

description: Scenario management is a proactive method which involves exploring different future hypotheses in a given field. It enables potential challenges to be anticipated and appropriate solutions to be developed. The use of an intelligence matrix facilitates the classification and analysis of relevant information for each scenario.

The generational filter is an analytical process that dissects the perceptions, behaviors, and forecasts of different generations. It combines digital and social research to understand the nuances of each generation, identify their values and motivations, and anticipate their trends and impacts. This process helps with strategic decision-making, particularly for administrator and decision-maker it allows for anticipating changes and adapting to future developments. However, it is important to note its limitations: risk of stereotypes, need for regular updates, influence of context, difficulty of long-term forecasting, and dependence on data quality.
instructions: Define the research question : Before starting the research, clearly define the research question and the objectives I want to achieve. Collect data : Use the generational filter to collect data specific to each generation. You could use different data sources, such as surveys, interviews, focus groups, online content analysis, Make sure to collect data representative of each generation. Analyze the info : Use qualitative and quantitative analysis methods to analyze the collected data. The generational filter would allow me to compare the responses and behaviors of different generations and identify significant trends and differences. Interpret the results : Interpret the research results taking into account the specificities of each generation. D'ont abuse generalizations and nuance my conclusions according to the context. Diffuse the results sing the generational filter in a rigorous and methodical way, I could conduct in-depth research and produce relevant and useful results.

description: Brand canon governance program (I66): owns the 13 BRAND_*.md canonicals, voice register modulation, jargon audit, drift-gate operations, and founder bio canonical maintenance. Per D-IH-66-R (sub-mark accountability), Brand Manager owns brand expression across all sub-marks; sub-mark delivery modes are owned by relevant existing function heads (Holistik Researcher / CMO+COO / CTO).

CORPINT-research operations program (I66): codifies the four HUMINT-derived SOPs (counterparty baseline assessment, elicitation discipline, reliability grading, intelligence report). Methodology adapted (not literally applied) from US Army FM 2-22.3 + ICD-203 public-release doctrine. Internal-register scope; per D-IH-66-M, never rendered to non-cleared audiences.

Per-engagement operations program (I66): discovery questionnaire, proposal generation, multi-cell engagement design. Per D-IH-66-R, no dedicated Engagement Manager role; processes split across Holistik Researcher (research-led discovery + multi-cell design) and Brand Manager (proposals, as brand-derived deliverables).
Brand legal operations program (I66): trademark monitoring (EUIPO + OEPM across umbrella + 3 sub-marks + 5 product marks per BRAND_HIERARCHY) and IP register maintenance.

Quarterly review of the 13 BRAND_*.md canonicals + cross-reference integrity + sub-mark / product reality check. Triggers tranche updates if a canonical is older than 12 months without a structural review. Governed by SOP-BRAND_CANON_MAINTENANCE_001.

CMO-accountable decision flow composing the three brand-name + domain governance silos into one path: brand-architecture fit (BRAND_ARCHITECTURE + BRAND_ABBREVIATIONS) then trademark clearance (SOP-TRADEMARK_NAMING_GOVERNANCE_001 for new sub-marks / product brands; Founder gate at Step 6) then domain availability + topology (SUBDOMAINS_REGISTRY + Vercel / Cloudflare) then make / buy / broker decision. RACI: A=CMO; R=Brand & Narrative Manager + Legal Counsel + System Owner / Domain Specialist; C=Founder + MKT/GTM; I=Operations. Governed by SOP-BRAND_DOMAIN_NAMING_001 + brand-naming-craft skill.

Monthly classification of soft-INFO signals from validate_brand_jargon.py + validate_brand_voice_register.py: each hit classified as accept-canon-change / require-source-fix / defer; quarterly strict-mode promotion review. Governed by SOP-BRAND_VOICE_DRIFT_TRIAGE_001.

Bi-annual review of BRAND_REGISTER_MATRIX.md + BRAND_BASELINE_REALITY_MATRIX.md per-audience entries; updates rows when an audience's signature pattern shifts. Governed by SOP-BRAND_REGISTER_MATRIX_REVIEW_001.
Quarterly review of BRAND_JARGON_AUDIT.md + BRAND_ABBREVIATIONS.md against newly observed external prose drift; updates forbidden / allowed token lists. Governed by SOP-BRAND_JARGON_AUDIT_REVIEW_001.

Pre-engagement structured assessment of counterparty (audience type, decision-maker shape, declared and inferred posture, known anti-patterns). Internal-register output; never rendered to counterparty. Governed by SOP-IO_COUNTERPARTY_BASELINE_ASSESSMENT_001.

Per-engagement elicitation plan: choice of approach techniques (direct interrogation / direct elicitation default / indirect elicitation / provocation), discovery questions across 5 sections, listening-protocol checklist, post-engagement follow-up cadence. Governed by SOP-IO_ELICITATION_DISCIPLINE_001.

Adapted-from-Admiralty A-F x 1-6 two-dimensional grading of every source consulted in an engagement (reliability x credibility). Internal-register; never rendered to counterparty literally; translated to high-confidence / working-hypothesis / open-question for external deliverables. Governed by SOP-IO_RELIABILITY_GRADING_001.

BLUF-led, source-graded internal-register report produced after a counterparty engagement (within 24h). Counterpart of the external-register research brief / engagement report. 6-section structure (BLUF / updated baseline / source-graded reliability / recommended next action / translation rules / cross-references). Governed by SOP-IO_INTELLIGENCE_REPORT_001.

External-register discovery questionnaire delivery; templated per audience (investor / SME / advisor / ENISA / partner / recruiter / customer). Per D-IH-66-R, role_owner is Holistik Researcher (research-led discovery); internal-register companion is B6 (elicitation). Governed by SOP-ENG_DISCOVERY_QUESTIONNAIRE_001. min/par/max effort hours match the discovery_synthesis method shape in SOP-ENG_ESTIMATION_DISCIPLINE_001 §3.

Templated proposal (scope + price posture + per-cell mapping into the SERVICE_OFFERING_CATALOG); produced after baseline assessment + elicitation cycle. Per D-IH-66-R, role_owner is Brand Manager (proposals are brand-derived deliverables; voice-tier-aligned per BRAND_REGISTER_MATRIX). Governed by SOP-ENG_PROPOSAL_001. min/par/max effort hours follow the proposal-author cycle calibrated against SOP-ENG_ESTIMATION_DISCIPLINE_001.

When the engagement spans multiple SERVICE_OFFERING_CATALOG cells, this process governs cell-to-cell transition design + per-phase deliverable lock + per-phase voice-tier consistency. Per D-IH-66-R, role_owner is Holistik Researcher (multi-cell methodology orchestration). Governed by SOP-ENG_ENGAGEMENT_DESIGN_001. min/par/max effort hours match design_specification in SOP-ENG_ESTIMATION_DISCIPLINE_001 §3.

Per-engagement effort × cost × duration triangle (min / par / max) generated by scripts/estimate_engagement.py from a scope.yaml + the canonical CSV trio (baseline_organisation rates, this process_list, and COUNTRY_WORK_CALENDAR). Governed by SOP-ENG_ESTIMATION_DISCIPLINE_001. role_owner is Project Manager (PMO).

Quarterly review of EUIPO + OEPM filing status for all Holistika marks (umbrella + 3 sub-marks + 5 product marks per BRAND_HIERARCHY). Includes opposition scan + renewal calendar + geographic-coverage gap detection. Governed by SOP-LEGAL_TRADEMARK_MONITORING_001.

Quarterly update of the IP register (registered marks, applications-in-progress, oppositions, renewals due). Companion to B12; together they form the brand-legal operational loop. Governed by SOP-LEGAL_IP_REGISTER_MAINTENANCE_001.

Quarterly review + update of FOUNDER_BIO.md canonical + per-audience FAQ + anonymised track-record. Drift-gate-checked against external register. Owned operationally by Talent (under CPO); lives under Brand Governance project because the bio is fundamentally a brand artefact. Governed by SOP-PEOPLE_FOUNDER_BIO_001.

Quarterly review of decks / dossiers / email signatures / press kit / recruiter copy / partner pitch / founder bio templates; ensures every template references canonical brand assets; operationalised via governance.brand_template_registry view (P6). Governed by SOP-BRAND_TEMPLATE_REGISTRY_MTNCE_001.

Quarterly review of the 4 brand drift gate signals (canon / jargon / voice / baseline-reality); promotes soft-INFO findings to hard-FAIL when source-fixes land; deprecates findings when the underlying canon changes. Governed by SOP-BRAND_DRIFT_GATE_OPS_001.

Per-introduction governance for adding new sub-marks or product brands to Holistika's trademark portfolio. 8-step process from naming candidate to filed mark; includes pre-search collision-risk assessment + formal clearance + filing strategy decision + filing prep + mandatory operator-pause-point at Step 6 + filing submission + post-filing handoff. Governed by SOP-TRADEMARK_NAMING_GOVERNANCE_001.
